generatedSentence: Dr. Aris Thorne, a grizzled hunter of ancient legends, found himself increasingly inspired by the strange phenomena plaguing the north and its nations. He knew it wasn't just folklore; something was tweaking the very fabric of reality, causing widespread hallucinations. His laboratory, lit by flickering lanterns, was a testament to years of investigation into things people mythify, fueled by an energy drink and an unyielding attitude. He rarely took a moment for leisure, driven by a deep fortitude and the insight that an immaterial force was causing a psychic attack. The local guild operated in lockstep, trying to uninstall the fear gripping the population nearby, but it was a losing battle against the growing psychic volt of a singular unit. He had an opportunity to press his theories further, even if it meant saying goodbye to his old life. The true source of the disruptions was a reclusive, power-hungry king whose personal vanity manifested as a massive gimmick to control the economy. He cultivated public envy through lavish displays, like grapes flown in daily. Citizens, constantly staring at distorted news, felt mocked by a new digital monarchy. A determined runner, running against the tide, tried to expose the truth, but was tackled amidst the gridlock of manipulated information. He had tried to reject the false narrative, but was heckled and pelted with tomatoes during a blackout. The king's control was absolute, a tightly knitted web, ensuring truths would simply disappear like phantom dragons. His jockey in the media made faces whitening with fear. The offense was clear. Working alone, Aris uncovered the billionaire's imperialism through metadata from his technology. He found the handwriting of a fable designed to create swollen profits, a true windfall of corrupted oil and synthetic berries. An expired warning, like a forgotten teabag, offered no resistance. The danger was lethal; facing a defendant in the village, a mere gesture could lead to a knockdown. He needed his intelligence to fight this flaming digital octopus, its tentacles spreading horizontal. He inserted his earplug against the noise, ready for the final confrontation. Aris’s quiet revolution began with a single webcast of truth, sparking immediate resistance. The corrupt system, a knitting of lies, began to fail randomly. The spokesman's blackboards were useless. The uniformed guards, once a scare, could no longer satisfy their masters. A rusty decker truck, driven by an old digger, arrived at the reclaimed rookery, bringing vegetable utensils to feed the hopeful. The cashier smiled. The grandmothers taught true geology, unlocking a new keyhole of understanding. A quiet lumen of hope, like a penguin waddling with purpose, finally synched with the future, ending the billiard game of power.
panelSentences: Dr. Aris Thorne, a grizzled hunter of ancient legends, found himself increasingly inspired by the strange phenomena plaguing the north and its nations. He knew it wasn't just folklore; something was tweaking the very fabric of reality, causing widespread hallucinations. His laboratory, lit by flickering lanterns, was a testament to years of investigation into things people mythify, fueled by an energy drink and an unyielding attitude. He rarely took a moment for leisure, driven by a deep fortitude and the insight that an immaterial force was causing a psychic attack. The local guild operated in lockstep, trying to uninstall the fear gripping the population nearby, but it was a losing battle against the growing psychic volt of a singular unit. He had an opportunity to press his theories further, even if it meant saying goodbye to his old life.

The true source of the disruptions was a reclusive, power-hungry king whose personal vanity manifested as a massive gimmick to control the economy. He cultivated public envy through lavish displays, like grapes flown in daily. Citizens, constantly staring at distorted news, felt mocked by a new digital monarchy. A determined runner, running against the tide, tried to expose the truth, but was tackled amidst the gridlock of manipulated information. He had tried to reject the false narrative, but was heckled and pelted with tomatoes during a blackout. The king's control was absolute, a tightly knitted web, ensuring truths would simply disappear like phantom dragons. His jockey in the media made faces whitening with fear. The offense was clear.

Working alone, Aris uncovered the billionaire's imperialism through metadata from his technology. He found the handwriting of a fable designed to create swollen profits, a true windfall of corrupted oil and synthetic berries. An expired warning, like a forgotten teabag, offered no resistance. The danger was lethal; facing a defendant in the village, a mere gesture could lead to a knockdown. He needed his intelligence to fight this flaming digital octopus, its tentacles spreading horizontal. He inserted his earplug against the noise, ready for the final confrontation.

Aris’s quiet revolution began with a single webcast of truth, sparking immediate resistance. The corrupt system, a knitting of lies, began to fail randomly. The spokesman's blackboards were useless. The uniformed guards, once a scare, could no longer satisfy their masters. A rusty decker truck, driven by an old digger, arrived at the reclaimed rookery, bringing vegetable utensils to feed the hopeful. The cashier smiled. The grandmothers taught true geology, unlocking a new keyhole of understanding. A quiet lumen of hope, like a penguin waddling with purpose, finally synched with the future, ending the billiard game of power.
rawPanelSentences: Dr. Aris Thorne, a grizzled **hunter** of ancient legends, found himself increasingly **inspired** by the strange phenomena plaguing the **north** and its **nations**. He knew it wasn't just folklore; something was **tweaking** the very fabric of reality, causing widespread **hallucinations**. His **laboratory**, lit by flickering **lanterns**, was a testament to years of **investigation** into things people **mythify**, fueled by an **energy drink** and an unyielding **attitude**. He **rarely** took a moment for **leisure**, driven by a deep **fortitude** and the **insight** that an **immaterial** force was causing a psychic **attack**. The local **guild** operated in **lockstep**, trying to **uninstall** the fear gripping the **population nearby**, but it was a losing battle against the growing psychic **volt** of a singular **unit**. He had an **opportunity** to **press** his theories further, even if it meant saying **goodbye** to his old life.

The true source of the disruptions was a reclusive, power-hungry **king** whose personal **vanity** manifested as a massive **gimmick** to control the **economy**. He cultivated public **envy** through lavish displays, like **grapes** flown in daily. Citizens, constantly **staring** at distorted news, felt **mocked** by a new digital **monarchy**. A determined **runner**, **running** against the tide, tried to expose the truth, but was **tackled** amidst the **gridlock** of manipulated information. He had tried to **reject** the false narrative, but was **heckled** and pelted with **tomatoes** during a **blackout**. The **king**'s control was absolute, a tightly **knitted** web, ensuring truths would simply **disappear** like phantom **dragons**. His **jockey** in the media made faces **whitening** with fear. The **offense** was clear.

Working **alone**, Aris uncovered the **billionaire**'s **imperialism** through **metadata** from his **technology**. He found the **handwriting** of a **fable** designed to create **swollen** profits, a true **windfall** of corrupted **oil** and synthetic **berries**. An **expired** warning, like a forgotten **teabag**, offered no **resistance**. The danger was **lethal**; facing a **defendant** in the **village**, a mere **gesture** could lead to a **knockdown**. He needed his **intelligence** to fight this **flaming** digital **octopus**, its tentacles spreading **horizontal**. He inserted his **earplug** against the noise, ready for the final confrontation.

Aris’s quiet **revolution** began with a single **webcast** of truth, sparking immediate **resistance**. The corrupt system, a **knitting** of lies, began to fail **randomly**. The **spokesman**'s **blackboards** were useless. The **uniformed** guards, once a **scare**, could no longer **satisfy** their masters. A **rusty** **decker** truck, driven by an old **digger**, arrived at the reclaimed **rookery**, bringing **vegetable** **utensils** to feed the hopeful. The **cashier** smiled. The **grandmothers** taught true **geology**, unlocking a new **keyhole** of understanding. A quiet **lumen** of hope, like a **penguin** waddling with purpose, finally **synched** with the future, ending the **billiard** game of power.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, gritty realistic graphic novel style, thick white gutters, clean black borders, cel-shading, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting. Protagonist: a grizzled man in his late 50s, with a neatly trimmed salt-and-pepper beard, wearing a worn leather jacket over a practical jumpsuit, and deep-set eyes lined with experience. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man in his dimly lit laboratory, surrounded by flickering lanterns and holographic data, intensely staring at a complex screen, a half-empty energy drink beside him. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A middle-aged man with sharp features and a manicured beard, wearing a tailored suit, sitting in a vast, dark control room, observing multiple screens displaying distorted news and phantom dragons, a sneer on his face. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The grizzled man alone in a rusty, abandoned village server farm, hiding behind a dilapidated wall, peering through a keyhole at a uniformed security guard. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The grizzled man standing, hands on hips, gazing out over a revitalized community, a 'decker' truck delivering fresh vegetables in the foreground, old grandmothers (visibly adult with wrinkles) tending to a small garden.

definitions.definition: To make mythical.
A sensory perception of something that does not exist, often arising from disorder of the nervous system, as in delirium tremens; a delusion.
The act of hallucinating; a wandering of the mind; an error, mistake or blunder.
To pinch and pull with a sudden jerk and twist; to twitch.
definitions.example: to tweak the nose.

definitions.definition: To adjust slightly; to fine-tune.
definitions.example: If we tweak the colors towards blue, it will look more natural.

definitions.definition: To twit or tease.
To abuse methamphetamines, especially crystal meth.
To exhibit symptoms of methamphetamine abuse, such as extreme nervousness, compulsiveness, erratic motion, excitability; possibly a blend of twitch and freak.
To exhibit extreme nervousness, evasiveness when confronted by law enforcement or other authority (e.g., customs agents, border patrol, teacher, etc.), mimicking methamphetamine abuse symptoms.
A tweak; a minor modification.
An attempt to cause damage, injury to, or death of opponent or enemy.
An attempt to detract from the worth or credibility of, a person, position, idea, object, or thing, by physical, verbal, emotional, or other assault.
definitions.example: They claimed the censorship of the article was an attack on free speech.

definitions.definition: A time in which one attacks; the offence of a battle.
definitions.example: The army timed their attack to coincide with the local celebrations.

definitions.definition: (by extension) The beginning of active operations on anything.
definitions.example: Having washed the plates from dinner, I made an attack on the laundry.

definitions.definition: An attempt to exploit a vulnerability in a computer system.
Collectively, the bowlers of a cricket side.
Any contact with the ball other than a serve or block which sends the ball across the plane of the net.
The three attackmen on the field or all the attackmen of a team.
The sudden onset of a disease or condition.
definitions.example: I've had an attack of the flu.

definitions.definition: An active episode of a chronic or recurrent disease.
The onset of a musical note, particularly with respect to the strength (and duration) of that onset.

(audio) The amount of time it takes for the volume of an audio signal to go from zero to maximum level (e.g. an audio waveform representing a snare drum hit would feature a very fast attack, whereas that of a wave washing to shore would feature a slow attack).
To apply violent force to someone or something.
definitions.example: This species of snake will only attack humans if it feels threatened.

definitions.definition: To aggressively challenge a person, idea, etc., with words (particularly in newspaper headlines, because it typesets into less space than "criticize" or similar).
definitions.example: She published an article attacking the recent pay cuts.

definitions.definition: To begin to affect; to act upon injuriously or destructively; to begin to decompose or waste.
To deal with something in a direct way; to set to work upon.
definitions.example: I attacked the meal with a hearty appetite.

definitions.definition: To aim balls at the batsman’s wicket.
To set a field, or bowl in a manner designed to get wickets.
To bat aggressively, so as to score runs quickly.
To move forward in an active attempt to score a point, as opposed to trying not to concede.
To accelerate quickly in an attempt to get ahead of the other riders.
The position of the body or way of carrying oneself.
definitions.example: The ballet dancer walked with a graceful attitude.

definitions.definition: Disposition or state of mind.
definitions.example: Don't give me your negative attitude.

definitions.definition: The orientation of a vehicle or other object relative to the horizon, direction of motion, other objects, etc.
definitions.example: The airliner had to land with a nose-up attitude after the incident.

definitions.definition: A position similar to arabesque, but with the raised leg bent at the knee.
To assume or to place in a particular position or orientation; to pose.
To express an attitude through one's posture, bearing, tone of voice, etc.
A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
A transvestite man, or more broadly a male-to-female transgender person.
To vanish.
To make vanish; especially, to abduct and murder surreptitiously for political reasons.
To go away; to become lost.
Effective management of a community or system, or especially its resources.
The study of money, currency and trade, and the efficient use of resources.
Frugal use of resources.
definitions.example: economy of word

definitions.definition: The system of production and distribution and consumption. The overall measure of a currency system; as the national economy.
The method of divine government of the world. (See w:Economy (religion).)
The part of a commercial passenger airplane or train reserved for those paying the lower standard fares; economy class.
Management of one’s residency.
Cheap to run; using minimal resources; representing good value for money.
definitions.example: Economy size.

definitions.definition: Resentful desire of something possessed by another or others (but not limited to material possessions).
An object of envious notice or feeling.
Hatred, enmity, ill-feeling.
Emulation; rivalry.
Public odium; ill repute.
To feel displeasure or hatred towards (someone) for their good fortune or possessions.
To have envious feelings (at).
To give (something) to (someone) grudgingly or reluctantly; to begrudge.
To show malice or ill will; to rail.
To do harm to; to injure; to disparage.
To hate.
To emulate.
A trick or device used to attain some end.
definitions.example: The box had a gimmick to make the coin appear to vanish.

definitions.definition: A clever ploy or strategy.
definitions.example: The contest was a gimmick to get people to sign up for their mailing list.

definitions.definition: A gimmick capacitor.
A night out with one's friends.
To rig or set up with a trick or device.
definitions.example: The magician's box was gimmicked with a wire that made it appear to open on its own.

definitions.definition: A small, round, smooth-skinned edible fruit, usually purple, red, or green, that grows in bunches on vines of genus Vitis.
A woody vine that bears clusters of grapes; a grapevine; of genus Vitis.
A dark purplish-red colour, the colour of many grapes.
Grapeshot.
A mangy tumour on a horse's leg.
A person's head.
To pick grapes.
(of livestock) To develop tubercules as a result of tuberculosis.
To develop a texture with small grape-like clusters of a contaminant or foreign substance.
(north) To grope.
One who rides racehorses competitively.
That part of a variable resistor or potentiometer that rides over the resistance wire
An operator of some machinery or apparatus.
A dealer in horses; a horse trader.
A cheat; one given to sharp practice in trade.
A prostitute's client.
A rapist.
To ride (a horse) in a race.
To jostle by riding against.
definitions.example: They were jockeying for position toward the end of the race.

definitions.definition: To maneuver (something) by skill for one's advantage.
To cheat or trick.
Having no matter or substance.
definitions.example: Because ghosts are immaterial, they can pass through walls.

definitions.definition: So insubstantial as to be irrelevant.
definitions.example: Objection, Your Honour! The defendant's criminal record is immaterial to this case.

definitions.definition: To infuse into the mind; to communicate to the spirit; to convey, as by a divine or supernatural influence; to disclose preternaturally; to produce in, as by inspiration.
To infuse into; to affect, as with a superior or supernatural influence; to fill with what animates, enlivens or exalts; to communicate inspiration to.
definitions.example: Elders should inspire children with sentiments of virtue.

definitions.definition: To draw in by the operation of breathing; to inhale.
To infuse by breathing, or as if by breathing.
To breathe into; to fill with the breath; to animate.
To spread rumour indirectly.
Having excellence through inspiration.
definitions.example: The actor's inspired performance of Hamlet's soliloquy left the audience dumbfounded.

definitions.definition: Filled with inspiration or motivated.
definitions.example: He was inspired to learn to fly.

definitions.definition: The act of investigating; the process of inquiring into or following up; research, especially patient or thorough inquiry or examination
definitions.example: Despite thorough investigation, the perpetrator of the attacks remains unknown.

definitions.definition: Quick to change one’s opinion or allegiance; insincere; not loyal or reliable.
Changeable.
A device used to apply pressure to an item.
definitions.example: a flower press

definitions.definition: A printing machine.
definitions.example: Stop the presses!

definitions.definition: (collective) The print-based media (both the people and the newspapers).
definitions.example: This article appeared in the press.

definitions.definition: A publisher.
An enclosed storage space (e.g. closet, cupboard).
definitions.example: Put the cups in the press.

definitions.definition: An exercise in which weight is forced away from the body by extension of the arms or legs.
(wagering) An additional bet in a golf match that duplicates an existing (usually losing) wager in value, but begins even at the time of the bet.
definitions.example: He can even the match with a press.

definitions.definition: Pure, unfermented grape juice.
definitions.example: I would like some Concord press with my meal tonight.

definitions.definition: A commission to force men into public service, particularly into the navy.
A crowd.
(construed with at) To look fixedly (at something).
To influence in some way by looking fixedly.
definitions.example: to stare a timid person into submission

definitions.definition: To be very conspicuous on account of size, prominence, colour, or brilliancy.
definitions.example: staring windows or colours

definitions.definition: To stand out; to project; to bristle.
The act of one who stares.
Shining; vivid, garish.
Looking fixedly with wide-open eyes.
Sensational, lurid.
(To cause) to become white or whiter; to bleach or blanch.
definitions.example: Age had whitened his hair.

definitions.definition: A substance, such as a bleach, used to make something white or whiter.
The process of making something white or whiter.
To move swiftly.
(fluids) To flow.
(of a vessel) To sail before the wind, in distinction from reaching or sailing close-hauled.
To control or manage, be in charge of.
definitions.example: He is running the candidate's expensive campaign.

definitions.definition: To be a candidate in an election.
definitions.example: I have decided to run for governor of California.

definitions.definition: To make run in a race or an election.
definitions.example: He ran his best horse in the Derby.

definitions.definition: To exert continuous activity; to proceed.
definitions.example: to run through life; to run in a circle

definitions.definition: To be presented in the media.
definitions.example: Her picture ran on the front page of the newspaper.

definitions.definition: To print or broadcast in the media.
definitions.example: run a story; run an ad

definitions.definition: To smuggle (illegal goods).
definitions.example: to run guns; to run rum

definitions.definition: To sort through a large volume of produce in quality control.
definitions.example: Looks like we're gonna have to run the tomatoes again.

definitions.definition: To extend or persist, statically or dynamically, through space or time.
To execute or carry out a plan, procedure or program.
definitions.example: Don't run that software unless you have permission.

definitions.definition: To pass or go quickly in thought or conversation.
definitions.example: to run from one subject to another

definitions.definition: To become different in a way mentioned (usually to become worse).
definitions.example: Our supplies are running low.

definitions.definition: To cost a large amount of money.
definitions.example: Buying a new laptop will run you a thousand dollars.

definitions.definition: Of stitches or stitched clothing, to unravel.
definitions.example: My stocking is running.

definitions.definition: To pursue in thought; to carry in contemplation.
To cause to enter; to thrust.
definitions.example: to run a sword into or through the body; to run a nail into one's foot

definitions.definition: To drive or force; to cause, or permit, to be driven.
To cause to be drawn; to mark out; to indicate; to determine.
definitions.example: to run a line

definitions.definition: To encounter or incur (a danger or risk).
definitions.example: to run the risk of losing one's life

definitions.definition: To put at hazard; to venture; to risk.
To tease with sarcasms and ridicule.
To sew (a seam) by passing the needle through material in a continuous line, generally taking a series of stitches on the needle at the same time.
To control or have precedence in a card game.
definitions.example: Every three or four hands he would run the table.

definitions.definition: To be in form thus, as a combination of words.
To be popularly known; to be generally received.
To have growth or development.
definitions.example: Boys and girls run up rapidly.

definitions.definition: To tend, as to an effect or consequence; to incline.
To have a legal course; to be attached; to continue in force, effect, or operation; to follow; to go in company.
definitions.example: Certain covenants run with the land.

definitions.definition: To encounter or suffer (a particular, usually bad, fate or misfortune).
To strike (the ball) in such a way as to cause it to run along the ground, as when approaching a hole.
To speedrun.
The action of the verb to run.
definitions.example: His running of the business leaves something to be desired.

definitions.definition: The activity of running as a form of exercise, as a sport, or for any other reason
definitions.example: Running is good exercise.

definitions.definition: That which runs or flows; the quantity of a liquid which flows in a certain time or during a certain operation.
definitions.example: the first running of a still

definitions.definition: The discharge from an ulcer or other sore.
Moving or advancing at a run.
Present, current.
definitions.example: running month

definitions.definition: Flowing; easy; cursive.
definitions.example: running handwriting

definitions.definition: Continuous; ongoing; keeping along step by step.
definitions.example: a running explanation

definitions.definition: Having a continuous design or pattern.
definitions.example: running bond; running ornament

definitions.definition: Consecutive.
Extending by a slender climbing or trailing stem.
definitions.example: a running vine

definitions.definition: Discharging pus.
definitions.example: a running sore

definitions.definition: (of a nose) Discharging snot or mucus.
definitions.example: a running nose

definitions.definition: Consecutively; in a row
definitions.example: Mom's strawberry jam won the blue ribbon at the Holland County Fair three years running.

definitions.definition: Approaching; about; roughly.
Not occurring at a regular interval; seldom; not often.
definitions.example: Rarely do you ever find an eagle this far up the river.

definitions.definition: Unusually well; excellently.
To a rare degree; very.
Something that is rejected.
An unpopular person.
A rejected defective product in a production line
To refuse to accept.
definitions.example: She even rejected my improved offer.

definitions.definition: To block a shot, especially if it sends the ball off the court.
To refuse a romantic advance.
definitions.example: I've been rejected three times this week.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of running, of moving rapidly using the feet.
definitions.example: I just got back from my morning run.

definitions.definition: Act or instance of hurrying (to or from a place) (not necessarily by foot); dash or errand, trip.
definitions.example: I need to make a run to the store.

definitions.definition: A pleasure trip.
definitions.example: Let's go for a run in the car.

definitions.definition: Flight, instance or period of fleeing.
Migration (of fish).
A group of fish that migrate, or ascend a river for the purpose of spawning.
A path taken by literal movement or figuratively
An enclosure for an animal; a track or path along which something can travel.
definitions.example: He set up a rabbit run.

definitions.definition: Rural landholding for farming, usually for running sheep, and operated by a runholder.
State of being current; currency; popularity.
Continuous or sequential
A flow of liquid; a leak.
definitions.example: The constant run of water from the faucet annoys me.

definitions.definition: (West Virginia) A small creek or part thereof. (Compare Southern US branch and New York and New England brook.)
definitions.example: The military campaign near that creek was known as "The battle of Bull Run".

definitions.definition: A quick pace, faster than a walk.
definitions.example: He broke into a run.

definitions.definition: A sudden series of demands on a bank or other financial institution, especially characterised by great withdrawals.
definitions.example: Financial insecurity led to a run on the banks, as customers feared for the security of their savings.

definitions.definition: Any sudden large demand for something.
definitions.example: There was a run on Christmas presents.

definitions.definition: Various horizontal dimensions or surfaces
A standard or unexceptional group or category.
definitions.example: He stood out from the usual run of applicants.

definitions.definition: In sports
A line of knit stitches that have unravelled, particularly in a nylon stocking.
definitions.example: I have a run in my stocking.

definitions.definition: The stern of the underwater body of a ship from where it begins to curve upward and inward.

The horizontal distance to which a drift may be carried, either by licence of the proprietor of a mine or by the nature of the formation; also, the direction which a vein of ore or other substance takes.
A pair or set of millstones.
(usually in the phrase 'do a runner') A quick escape away from a scene.
definitions.example: He did a runner after robbing the drugstore.

definitions.definition: A deserter.
A type of soft-soled shoe originally intended for runners.
Part of a shoe that is stitched to the bottom of the upper so it can be glued to the sole.
A part of an apparatus that moves quickly.
definitions.example: After the cycle completes, the runner travels back quickly to be in place for the next cycle.

definitions.definition: A mechanical part intended to guide or aid something else to move (using wheels or sliding).
An automobile; a working or driveable automobile.
definitions.example: Is that old Mercedes on the forecourt a runner? / No, it has no gearbox.

definitions.definition: A strip of fabric used to decorate or protect a table or dressing table.
definitions.example: The red runner makes the table so festive.

definitions.definition: A long, narrow carpet for a high traffic area such as a hall or stairs.
definitions.example: How about we put down a clear runner in the front hall.

definitions.definition: A part of a cigarette that is burning unevenly.
A long stolon sent out by a plant (such as strawberry), in order to root new plantlets, or a plant that propagates by using such runners.
A short sling with a carabiner on either end, used to link the climbing rope to a bolt or other protection such as a nut or friend.
A competitor in a poker tournament.
A restaurant employee responsible for taking food from the kitchens to the tables.
A leaping food fish (Elagatis pinnulatis) of Florida and the West Indies; the skipjack, shoemaker, or yellowtail.
(sports slang) An employee of a sports agent who tries to recruit possible player clients for the agent.
A rope to increase the power of a tackle.
A speedrunner.
An idea or plan that has potential to be adopted or put into operation.
definitions.example: This idea isn't a runner. Let's not waste any more time on it.

definitions.definition: To mimic, to simulate.
To create an artistic representation of.
To make fun of by mimicking, to taunt.
To tantalise, and disappoint the hopes of.
To create a mockup or prototype of.
definitions.example: What's the best way to mock a database layer?

definitions.definition: A government in which sovereignty is embodied within a single, today usually hereditary head of state (whether as a figurehead or as a powerful ruler).
The territory ruled over by a monarch; a kingdom.

A form of government where sovereignty is embodied by a single ruler in a state and his high aristocracy representing their separate divided lands within the state and their low aristocracy representing their separate divided fiefs.
States based on a system of governance headed by a king or a queen.
One who hunts game for sport or for food; a huntsman or huntswoman.
A dog used in hunting.
A horse used in hunting, especially a thoroughbred, bred and trained for hunting.
One who hunts or seeks after anything.
definitions.example: The hunter becomes the hunted.

definitions.definition: A person who bottles up their aggression and eventually releases it explosively.
A kind of spider, the huntsman or hunting spider.
A pocket watch with a spring-hinged circular metal cover that closes over the dial and crystal, protecting them from dust and scratches.
To force a person to the ground with the weight of one's own body, usually by jumping on top or slamming one's weight into him or her.
To face or deal with, attempting to overcome or fight down.

definitions.definition: To attempt to take away a ball.
To bring a ball carrier to the ground.
To "hit on" or pursue a person that one is interested in.
Made of ropes tackled together.

In the International System of Units, the derived unit of electrical potential and electromotive force (voltage); the potential difference across a conductor when a current of one ampere uses one watt of power. Symbol: V
That which is vain, futile, or worthless; that which is of no value, use or profit.
Excessive pride in or admiration of one's own abilities, appearance or achievements.

A dressing table used to apply makeup, preen, and coif hair. The table is normally quite low and similar to a desk, with drawers and one or more mirrors on top. Either a chair or bench is used to sit upon.
A washbasin installed into a permanently fixed storage unit, used as an item of bathroom furniture.
Emptiness.
Any idea, theory or statement that is without foundation.

definitions.definition: A particular, minute unit of mass, defined differently for different substances, but so that varying substances of the same general type have the property that one international unit of the one has the same effect on the human body as one international unit of the other.
Oneness, singularity, seen as a component of a whole number; a magnitude of one.
A standard measure of a quantity.
definitions.example: The centimetre is a unit of length.

definitions.definition: The number one.
An organized group comprising people and/or equipment.
definitions.example: He was a member of a special police unit.

definitions.definition: A member of a military organization.
definitions.example: The fifth tank brigade moved in with 20 units. (i.e., 20 tanks)

definitions.definition: Any military element whose structure is prescribed by competent authority, such as a table of organization and equipment; specifically, part of an organization.
An organization title of a subdivision of a group in a task force.
A standard or basic quantity into which an item of supply is divided, issued or detailed. In this meaning, also called unit of issue.

With regard to Reserve Components of the Armed Forces, denotes a Selected Reserve unit organized, equipped, and trained for mobilization to serve on active duty as a unit or to augment or be augmented by another unit. Headquarters and support functions without wartime missions are not considered units.
The identity element, neutral element.
An element having an inverse, an invertible element; an associate of the unity.
In an adjunction, a natural transformation from the identity functor of the domain of the left adjoint functor to the composition of the right adjoint functor with the left adjoint functor.

A volume of rock or ice of identifiable origin and age range that is defined by the distinctive and dominant, easily mapped and recognizable petrographic, lithologic or paleontologic features (facies) that characterize it.
An item which may be sold singly.

definitions.definition: A unit of alcohol.
One kilowatt-hour (as recorded on an electricity meter).

A measure of housing equivalent to the living quarters of one household; an apartment where a group of apartments is contained in one or more multi-storied buildings or a group of dwellings is in one or more single storey buildings, usually arranged around a driveway.
A gold coin of the reign of James I, worth twenty shillings.
A work unit.
A physically large person.
For each unit.
definitions.example: We have to keep our unit costs down if we want to make a profit.

definitions.definition: Having a size or magnitude of one.
A highly caffeinated, sometimes carbonated, beverage containing added ingredients advertised as increasing alertness, performance etc.
A male monarch; a man who heads a monarchy. If it's an absolute monarchy, then he is the supreme ruler of his nation.

definitions.definition: A powerful or majorly influential person.
definitions.example: Howard Stern styled himself as the "king of all media".

definitions.definition: Something that has a preeminent position.

definitions.definition: A component of certain games.
A king skin.

definitions.definition: A male dragonfly; a drake.
A king-sized bed.
A male person.
To crown king, to make (a person) king.
To rule over as king.
To perform the duties of a king.
To assume or pretend preeminence (over); to lord it over.
To promote a piece of draughts/checkers that has traversed the board to the opposite side, that piece subsequently being permitted to move backwards as well as forwards.
To dress and perform as a drag king.
To turn thread or yarn into a piece of fabric by forming loops that are pulled through each other. This can be done by hand with needles or by machine.

definitions.definition: To join closely and firmly together.

definitions.definition: To become closely and firmly joined; become compacted.
To grow together.

definitions.definition: To combine from various elements.

definitions.definition: Of bones: to heal following a fracture.

definitions.definition: To form into a knot, or into knots; to tie together, as cord; to fasten by tying.
To draw together; to contract into wrinkles.
Made by knitting, or resembling in texture something made by knitting.
A condition of total, interlocking traffic congestion on the streets or highways of a crowded city, in which no one can move because everyone is in someone else's way.

On a smaller scale: the situation in which cars enter a signal-controlled intersection too late during the green light cycle, and are unable to clear the intersection (due to congestion in the next block) when the light turns red, thus blocking the cross traffic when it's their turn to go. Repeated at enough intersections, this phenomenon can lead to citywide gridlock.
(by extension) any paralysis of a complex system due to severe congestion, conflict, or deadlock.
To cause traffic congestion
A room, building or institution equipped for scientific research, experimentation or analysis.
A place where chemicals, drugs or microbes are prepared or manufactured.
A case of translucent or transparent material made to protect a flame, or light, used to illuminate its surroundings.
Especially, a metal casing with lens used to illuminate a stage (e.g. spotlight, floodlight).
An open structure of light material set upon a roof, to give light and air to the interior.
A cage or open chamber of rich architecture, open below into the building or tower which it crowns.
A smaller and secondary cupola crowning a larger one, for ornament, or to admit light.

definitions.definition: A lantern pinion or trundle wheel.

(steam engines) A kind of cage inserted in a stuffing box and surrounding a piston rod, to separate the packing into two parts and form a chamber between for the reception of steam, etc.; a lantern brass.
A light formerly used as a signal by a railway guard or conductor at night.
A perforated barrel to form a core upon.
Aristotle's lantern
A sight or view of the interior of anything; a deep inspection or view; introspection; frequently used with into.
Power of acute observation and deduction
Knowledge (usually derived from consumer understanding) that a company applies in order to make a product or brand perform better and be more appealing to customers
Intuitive apprehension of the inner nature of a thing or things; intuition.
An extended understanding of a subject resulting from identification of relationships and behaviors within a model, context, or scenario.
An individual's awareness of the nature and severity of one's mental illness.
The act of resisting, or the capacity to resist.

definitions.definition: A force that tends to oppose motion.
Electrical resistance.
An underground organisation engaged in a struggle for liberation from forceful occupation; a resistance movement.
Liquid fat.
Petroleum-based liquid used as fuel or lubricant.
An oil painting.
Oil paint.

definitions.definition: A small succulent fruit, of any one of many varieties.
A soft fruit which develops from a single ovary and contains seeds not encased in pits.
A coffee bean.
One of the ova or eggs of a fish.
(African American) A police car.
A dollar.
To pick berries.

definitions.definition: To bear or produce berries.
A mound; a barrow.
A burrow, especially a rabbit's burrow.
An excavation; a military mine.
To beat; give a beating to; thrash.
To thresh (grain).
A temporary loss of consciousness.
A temporary loss of memory.
An instance of censorship, especially a temporary one.

definitions.definition: The mandatory blocking of all light emanating from buildings as imposed during World War II.
To question harshly in an attempt to find or reveal weaknesses.
To insult, tease, make fun of or badger.

definitions.definition: To prepare flax for spinning using special combs called hackles
A widely cultivated plant, Solanum lycopersicum, having edible fruit.

A shade of red, the colour of a ripe tomato.
A desirable-looking woman.

definitions.definition: A stupid act or person.

A corporation.

Close connection, unison, rigid synchronization.

Acquaintance; intercourse; familiarity.

Physical strength.
The act of offending:

The flesh of these marine molluscs eaten as food.
An organization that has many powerful branches controlled from the centre.
To put (or attempt to put) one's fingers, hands or arms in many things or places at roughly the same time.
To spread out in long arms or legs in many directions.

Text that was written by hand.
The characteristic writing of a particular person.

A historically constituted, stable community of people, formed on the basis of a common language, territory, economic life, ethnicity and/or psychological make-up manifested in a common culture.

definitions.definition: A sovereign state.

definitions.definition: An association of students based on its members' birthplace or ethnicity.

definitions.definition: A great number; a great deal.
One of the four major compass points, specifically 0°, directed toward the North Pole, and conventionally upwards on a map, abbreviated as N.

definitions.definition: The up or positive direction.

definitions.definition: The positive or north pole of a magnet, which seeks the magnetic pole near Earth's geographic North Pole (which, for its magnetic properties, is a south pole).
To turn or move toward the north.
Of or pertaining to the north; northern.

definitions.definition: Toward the north; northward.

definitions.definition: More or greater than.

definitions.definition: Toward the north; northward; northerly.

Farewell; a formula used to another person or persons when the speaker, writer, or person addressed is departing.
Freedom provided by the cessation of activities.
Free time, time free from work or duties.
Time at one's command, free from engagement; convenient opportunity; hence, convenience; ease.

Fiction; untruth; falsehood.

definitions.definition: Protuberant or abnormally distended (as by injury or disease).
Something that has been blown down by the wind.

Horizon

Relating to horizontal markets
Pertaining to the horizon

Of an interval: having the two notes sound successively.
By oneself, solitary.

definitions.definition: Apart from, or exclusive of, others.

definitions.definition: Considered separately.
Without equal.
Unique; rare; matchless.
By oneself; apart from, or exclusive of, others; solo.

definitions.definition: Without outside help.

definitions.definition: Exclusively.

To fit with earplugs.
To die.

That is no longer valid

definitions.definition: Very bright and the color of flame.
Extremely obvious; visibly evident. Typically of a homosexual male.

definitions.definition: Damned, bloody.

To synchronize, especially in the senses of data synchronization, time synchronization, or synchronizing music with video.
definitions.example: Our cloud storage automatically syncs your files to all your devices.

definitions.definition: To flush all pending I/O operations to disk.
To dismiss (someone, especially military personnel) from service
To discard, put away
To annul
A video and/or audio broadcast transmitted via the Internet
To make such a broadcast
Marked or corroded by rust.
Of the rust color, reddish or reddish-brown.
Lacking recent experience, out of practice, especially with respect to a skill or activity.
Of clothing, especially dark clothing: worn, shabby.
Affected with the fungal plant disease called rust.
One who, or that which, decks or adorns; a coverer.
definitions.example: a table decker

definitions.definition: (used in conjunction with a number) Something having numerous levels.
definitions.example: We traveled in a double-decker bus.
